title: Explore the Best Beaches in Albania by Car from TIA
excerpt: Discover Albania’s Riviera by rental car from Tirana Airport, with driving distances, road tips and the top beach spots for a relaxed coastal adventure.
keywords: best beaches in Albania by car
intro: Landing at Tirana International Airport? A rental car gives you the freedom to chase the turquoise waters and golden sands of the Albanian Riviera at your own pace.

sections.title: Best Beaches in Albania by Car: Why the Riviera Shines
sections.body: The Albanian Riviera stretches over 200 km of coastline, offering a mix of bustling bays and hidden coves. With a car you can hop from one spot to the next, avoiding the limited bus schedules that often skip the smaller beaches. From the long, sandy expanse of Borsh to the crystal‑clear waters of Ksamil, the route lets you tailor your day‑trip itinerary. This flexibility is why the phrase “best beaches in Albania by car” resonates with travelers seeking both convenience and adventure.

sections.title: The Scenic Llogara Pass to Himarë
sections.body: From TIA, the drive to Himarë is about 220 km and takes you through the dramatic Llogara Pass. The winding road climbs to 1,027 m, offering panoramic views of the Ionian Sea on one side and the rugged mountains on the other. Expect a mix of well‑maintained asphalt and occasional narrow sections, especially after rain. Stop at the Llogara National Park viewpoint for a coffee break, then continue down the coastal road where signage is clear and parking is available near the town’s promenade.

sections.title: Southern Gems: Sarandë and Ksamil
sections.body: Continuing south, Sarandë lies roughly 280 km from the airport. The drive takes around 4 hours, mostly on a modern highway that runs parallel to the coast. From Sarandë, a short 15‑minute ride brings you to Ksamil, famous for its white sand and turquoise lagoons. Both towns have ample parking lots, but peak summer months can fill them quickly. Visiting in May or late September lets you enjoy the beaches with fewer crowds, and the roads remain smooth, making day trips to nearby coves easy with a rental car.

sections.title: Secluded Gems: Borsh and Gjipë
sections.body: For travelers craving solitude, Borsh offers a 7 km continuous beach that stays relatively uncrowded even in high season. A short detour off the main highway leads to a modest parking area near the village. Further east, Gjipë Beach is a hidden treasure; you’ll park at a clifftop lot and walk about 1.5 km down a rocky trail to reach the pebbly shore. The road to Gjipë is narrower and less frequented, so a reliable vehicle from AutoConnect Rental ensures you can handle the occasional gravel patches without worry.

tip.title: Pro Tip: Park Smart, Walk Smart
tip.body: When you reach popular spots like Ksamil, arrive early in the morning to secure a parking space close to the water. For remote beaches such as Gjipë, bring sturdy shoes and a small backpack with water— the final walk down the trail is steep and can be slippery after rain.

faq.question: Can you easily reach the best beaches in the Albanian Riviera without a car?
faq.answer: Public transport exists but runs infrequently and often skips the smaller coves. A rental car provides the most flexible and time‑efficient way to visit both well‑known and hidden beaches along the Riviera.

faq.question: How long is the drive from Tirana Airport to Sarandë and Ksamil?
faq.answer: The journey to Sarandë is about 280 km and takes roughly 4 hours on the main coastal highway. From Sarandë, Ksamil is an additional 15‑minute, 12‑km drive.

faq.question: What are the road conditions when driving to remote beaches like Gjipë?
faq.answer: The main route is paved, but the final stretch to Gjipë involves a narrower, occasionally gravelled road followed by a 1.5 km clifftop walk. A well‑maintained rental car handles the approach comfortably.
